LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT ALLEN BYERLY'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. BOTH THE DEFAULT AND DEFAULT JUDGMENT ENTERED AGAINST MR. BYERLY ARE SET ASIDE. MR. BYERLY HAS MADE A SUFFICIENT SHOWING TO ENTITLE HIM TO THE RELIEF HE SEEKS PER CCP 473(B) AND GRANTING THAT RELIEF ACCORDS WITH CALIFORNIA'S STRONG POLICY IN FAVOR OF DECIDING CASES ON THEIR MERITS. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: IRENE TOFFT, CSR # 12313

MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
Off Calendar Jul-05-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT ALLEN BYERLY's MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS. Defendant Allen Byerly's motion to quash service of summons is granted. No opposition filed. Order is signed. Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR 711, ckaren@sftc.org =(302/HK)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Default Judgment
Off Calendar May-31-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANT ALLEN BYERLY'S Motion To Set Aside Default Judgment is Granted. Both the default and default judgment entered against Mr. Byerly are set aside. Mr. Byerly has made a sufficient showing to entitle him to the relief he seeks per CCP 473(b) and granting that relief accords with California's strong policy in favor of deciding cases on their merits. Order signed. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Irene Tofft, CSR # 12313 =302/hk
